title: Name table ('N')
text: Name table ('N') Construct the name of the overflow table. Description Parse the table name to determine the position of the ‘O’ indicating an overflow table. The purpose of the Name table request is: When uinfo->tabname is set to 2 in the Info request, this request is used to determine the position of the ‘O’ indicating an overflow table. To change the table name. If an occurrence of an entity containing variable-length fields grows to more than 8192 bytes, Uniface creates overflow segments that are stored in overflow tables. The overflow table or file has the same name as the ‘main’ entity, preceded by an initial ‘O’. The syntax of the table names can be too complex to let Uniface place this ‘O’. An example of this is when database, catalogs, database schema names and separators for these items are included in the entity name. In this case, the Name table request can be used to let the connector place the ‘O’. Uniface will only issue this request to the DBMS connector when uinfo->tabname is set to 2 in the Info request. From Uniface Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->umisc Pointer to the table name. To Uniface Table 2. Information Supplied to Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->iobuf When the DBMS connector wants to return a new table name, it has to provide Uniface with this name in uctrl->iobuf . When this is not the case, the DBMS connector has to make sure that uctrl->iobuf[0] contains ‘/0’. The position where the ‘O’ should be placed in case of an overflow table. Table 3. Connector Request Return Values Value Description >=0 The position to place the ‘O’ in case of overflow tables. -9 I/O server logon error. The connector request return value must be 0. Do not change the value of uctrl->udrverr . Extra information The Name table request is provided with a dummy control block. This control block does not describe a table and it does not have an associated connector workspace. Related Links uinfo Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector. uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.
